content:
There is **no shipment feed** because **no logistics/supply-chain documents were uploaded**.

Here's everything that's been parsed:

| Type | Docs |
|------|------|
| **Specifications** | 26 33 53 (UPS), 23 64 26 (Chillers), 21 22 00 (Fire Suppression) — plus 3 HTML versions |
| **Submittals** | SUB-212200-01-R0, SUB-236426-01-R0, SUB-263353-01-R0, SUB-263353-02-R0 |
| **Addendum** | ADD‑001.pdf |
| **ERP POs / Shipments / Logistics** | **None** |

The ontology only builds what it receives. To get shipment tracking, PO status, or logistics feeds, you'd need to upload:

- ERP purchase orders (PO‑4500012345 etc.)
- Shipping manifests / packing lists
- Logistics tracking feeds
- Vendor dispatch / delivery receipts

No such documents → no shipments, no POs, no supply chain pages.
reasoning_content:
The user is asking why there's no shipment feed/page. Looking at the project_overview, the site_pages listed don't include any "supply chain", "shipment", "logistics", etc. page. The documents uploaded are:

1. 3 addenda/spec documents (SPEC_212200_FIRE, SPEC_236426_CHILLERS, SPEC_263353_UPS)
2. 4 submittal documents (SUB-212200-01-R0, SUB-236426-01-R0, SUB-263353-01-R0, SUB-263353-02-R0)
3. 3 HTML spec documents (spec_21_22_00, spec_23_64_26, spec_26_33_53)
4. ADD-001.pdf

No ERP purchase orders, no logistics tracking, no shipment feeds, no vendor PO documents were uploaded. The system only parses what's uploaded, so there are no PO objects, shipment objects, or logistics data because no such documents were provided.
